imports.notes: Always import `from manim_slides import Slide` (not ThreeDSlide unless 3D content is needed). `from pathlib import Path` is used when referencing external image files via Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / '...'.

class_structure.construct_method: def construct(self): - contains all slide content with sections separated by self.next_slide(). Local helper functions (make_box, shrink_to_fit_width) are defined at the top of construct() for reuse within the slide.
class_structure.example:
class DDPGSlide(Slide):
    def construct(self):
        def make_box(mobject, color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.12, stroke_width=1.5):
            box = RoundedRectangle(
                corner_radius=0.2,
                width=mobject.width + 0.8,
                height=mobject.height + 0.6,
                color=color,
                fill_opacity=fill_opacity,
                stroke_width=stroke_width,
            )
            box.move_to(mobject)
            return box

        title = Text("DDPG: ...", font_size=38, color=BLUE_B)
        title.to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(title))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.next_slide()

colors.equation_color: WHITE (default), GREEN for update rules, ORANGE for alternatives

color_semantics.WHITE: body prose, main content text — default for unlabeled content
color_semantics.GRAY_A: subdued/secondary text, advisor/author lines, descriptive annotations
color_semantics.GREEN: definitions, positive properties, algorithm outputs, update rules — usable on labels AND equation sub-expressions
color_semantics.ORANGE: alternatives, exploration, covariance, local controllers, dual/secondary concepts — usable on labels AND equation sub-expressions
color_semantics.YELLOW: inline highlights for specific variables or key terms being introduced within a slide (e.g., Lagrange multipliers λ, ν)
color_semantics.highlight_scope_rule: Accent colors apply to the specific term or label carrying semantic weight. Body prose and full sentences stay WHITE. Never color an entire paragraph with an accent color.

typography.equation_font_size: 22-36 (matrices/long eqs: 19-24; use shrink_to_fit_width for very long)

layout.content_area: below title, centered horizontally; section labels at UP*2.5 or UP*2
layout.section_label_position: move_to(UP * 2.5) or move_to(UP * 2) for most sections; next_to(title, DOWN, buff=0.5) less common
layout.margin_notes: content uses buff=0.3-0.5 between elements; boxes add 0.8 to width, 0.5-0.6 to height of content
layout.width_guard: Use shrink_to_fit_width(eq, config.frame_width - 1.5) for equations that may overflow

entry_animations: Write (only for progressive text reveal in early slides 01-06)
Create (for diagrams, connections, neural network wires)
animations.equation_reveal: FadeIn complete equation, or reveal sub-VGroups sequentially with separate self.play() calls
animations.transitions: self.wait(0.5) then self.next_slide() after each content reveal
animations.cleanup_pattern: FadeOut all section mobjects before next section: self.play(FadeOut(...), FadeOut(...)); self.wait(0.3)
animations.within_section_swap: FadeOut current box+content, self.wait(0.2), FadeIn next box+content (no next_slide between swaps)
animations.3d_usage: ThreeDSlide only for 3D visualizations; most slides use standard Slide

equations.alignment_pattern: Use & for alignment in multiline equations. Use VGroup(...).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.18-0.25, aligned_edge=LEFT) for bullet-point equation lists.
equations.labeling: No numbered equation labels. Use descriptive Text labels positioned above or below.
equations.color_highlighting: Set color on the full MathTex call OR index into sub-expressions: eq[0].set_color(GREEN). Semantic accent colors (GREEN, ORANGE, YELLOW, etc.) may be used on any label, Text mobject, or equation sub-expression that carries a specific semantic role — not limited to equations. Structural highlight boxes use BLUE_B (fill_opacity=0.12, stroke_width=2). Body prose Text always stays WHITE.

highlight_box.use_case: Key equations, control laws, final results

reusable_components.name: make_box local helper
reusable_components.description: Creates a RoundedRectangle centered on the given mobject. Arguments: mobject, color=GRAY, fill_opacity=0.15, stroke_width=1. Returns the box.
reusable_components.pattern:
def make_box(mobject, color=GRAY, fill_opacity=0.15, stroke_width=1):
    box = RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.2, width=mobject.width+0.8, height=mobject.height+0.6, color=color, fill_opacity=fill_opacity, stroke_width=stroke_width)
    box.move_to(mobject)
    return box

reusable_components.name: shrink_to_fit_width local helper
reusable_components.description: Scales a mobject down if it exceeds max_width. Returns the mobject. Use with config.frame_width - 1.5 for full-width equations.

reusable_components.name: Neural network diagram
reusable_components.description: Fully connected NN diagram built from Circle neurons and Line connections, with layer VGroups. Layer colors: BLUE (input), PURPLE (hidden), GREEN (output).

reusable_components.name: Equation-label VGroup pattern
reusable_components.description: Pair an equation with a descriptive Text label: VGroup(label, eq).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.15). Used for labeled equations throughout 13_ilqr.py and others.

json_slide_files.purpose: Auto-generated by manim-slides during rendering. Maps each self.next_slide() call to an MP4 segment. Never edit manually.
json_slide_files.structure_notes: Top-level keys: 'slides' (array of segment objects), 'resolution': [1920, 1080], 'background_color': 'black'. Each segment: loop, auto_next, playback_rate, reversed_playback_rate, notes, dedent_notes, skip_animations, src (null), type ('video'), direction ('horizontal'), file (path to .mp4), rev_file (path to reversed .mp4).
json_slide_files.filename_pattern: Same base name as the scene class, e.g. DDPGSlide.json for class DDPGSlide in 14_ddpg.py. File path: slides/files/<ClassName>/<hash>.mp4

naming_conventions.scene_class: CamelCase ending in 'Slide', e.g. QLearningSlide, ILQRSlide, DDPGSlide
naming_conventions.variable_naming: snake_case for variables, PascalCase for Mobjects (e.g. trayectoria_box, motivation_group)
naming_conventions.multiple_scenes_per_file: Allowed — list each scene class separately in slides.toml
naming_conventions.docstring: Module-level docstring required: describes purpose, includes Example: block with render command

canonical_style.section_cleanup: FadeOut all mobjects before next section

do_not_break: Use `from manim import *` and `from manim_slides import Slide` as the only required imports
Class name must end with 'Slide' and subclass Slide
Title must use Text() with color=BLUE_B, font_size=38-42, positioned to_edge(UP, buff=0.5), animated with FadeIn
Every logical content reveal must end with self.wait(0.5) followed by self.next_slide()
Use FadeIn for all content entry in canonical style (07+); never Write for new slides
All visible text must be in Spanish
Wrap definitions, theorems, key equations, and bullet point lists in RoundedRectangle boxes with color=BLUE_D, fill_opacity=0.12, stroke_width=1.5

Every mathematical expression — equations, variables, Greek letters, subscripts, superscripts, operators, inline symbols — must be rendered with MathTex or Tex. Never embed math inside a plain Text() object, even for single symbols like θ, λ, x_t. Mix prose and math using VGroup(Text(...), MathTex(...)) arranged horizontally, or Tex with \text{} for the prose portion.
Background must be BLACK (#000000); never set background_color explicitly in code (configured in slides.toml)
Resolution is 1080p@60fps as configured in slides.toml — never set camera frame size in code
Section cleanup: FadeOut ALL mobjects from a section before starting the next section
Never add new scenes to an existing file without also updating slides.toml
Never manually edit .json slide files — they are auto-generated
Use VGroup for related mobjects with .arrange() for layout
Use config.frame_width - 1.5 as the max_width guard for wide equations
Module-level docstring is required in every new file

style_inconsistencies.recommendation: Use BLUE_B for titles in all new slides and updates (reference: 00_portrait.py)
Use FadeIn for cleaner slide transitions
Use 0.5s wait after FadeIn for pacing

style_inconsistencies.canonical: Include module-level docstring with description and Example: render command
style_inconsistencies.recommendation: Always include docstrings in new slide files

style_inconsistencies.pattern: AgentEnvironmentSlide uses self.add(title) and Rectangle (not RoundedRectangle)
style_inconsistencies.recommendation: 07 is a transitional file with heavier custom layout; follow 11-14 patterns for new slides

slides_toml_reference.slides_array: List of scene paths in presentation order, e.g. 'slides.14_ddpg.DDPGSlide'
